mcqs.question: What is a key characteristic of a Java constructor?

options.text: It has a return type of void
It has the same name as the class and no return type
It can be used to return values
It is only used for abstract classes

mcqs.explanation: A Java constructor has the same name as the class and does not have a return type, not even void. This is a fundamental aspect of constructors in Java.

mcqs.question: When does Java provide a default constructor for a class?

options.text: Always
When no constructors are explicitly defined
Only for abstract classes
Never

mcqs.explanation: Java provides a default constructor only when no constructors are explicitly defined for a class. This default constructor does nothing and is essentially an empty constructor.

mcqs.question: What is constructor overloading in Java?

options.text: Overriding a method in a subclass
Defining multiple constructors with different parameter lists
Calling a constructor multiple times
Using the super keyword to call a superclass constructor

mcqs.explanation: Constructor overloading in Java involves defining multiple constructors for a class, each with a different parameter list. This allows for more flexibility in how objects of the class can be created.

mcqs.question: Where must the this() keyword appear in a constructor?

options.text: As the last statement
Anywhere in the constructor
As the first statement
Outside of a constructor

mcqs.explanation: The this() keyword must appear as the first statement in a constructor. It is used to call another constructor of the same class, allowing for constructor chaining.

mcqs.question: How does Java handle object destruction?

options.text: Through the use of destructors
With the delete keyword
Via automatic garbage collection
Manually, through memory deallocation

mcqs.explanation: Java handles object destruction automatically through its garbage collection mechanism. This frees the developer from worrying about manually deallocating memory.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of constructor chaining in Java?

options.text: To call a method of the superclass
To create a new object of a different class
To call another constructor of the same class
To override a method of the superclass

mcqs.explanation: Constructor chaining in Java is used to call another constructor of the same class. This is achieved using the this() keyword and allows for more flexibility in how objects are created.

mcqs.question: Can a constructor in Java have a return type?

options.text: Yes, any return type is allowed
Yes, but only void is allowed
No, constructors cannot have a return type
It depends on the specific class

mcqs.explanation: Constructors in Java cannot have a return type, not even void. This is a fundamental aspect of the language and distinguishes constructors from regular methods.
